meta.title: Virus RNA and Protein Synthesis in Cells Infected with Different Strains of Newcastle Disease Virus

abstractHtml: The virus RNAs and proteins synthesized in chick embryo cells infected with different strains of Newcastle disease virus have been characterized by polyacrylamide gel electrophoresis. By comparison of the rates of electrophoretic mobility of virus RNA with those of RNA molecules of known molecular weight, the molecular weights were estimated to be 4.8 x 106 for the virus particle RNA and 2.5 x 106, 1.1 x 106 and 0.4 to 0.7 x 106 for the RNA molecules synthesized in infected cells, the latter being heterogeneous. Synthesis of a number of virus particle proteins was readily detected.
